7:30 P.M.- 9:30 P.M.
Palacio Ferreyra 
An architectural landmark in the heart of Córdoba, the Palacio Ferreyra is a French-style mansion built in the early 20th century and home 
to the Evita Fine Arts Museum. Surrounded by gardens, the museum houses an impressive collection of Argentine art from the 19th century 
to the present, offering visitors a unique blend of history, culture, and artistic expression.

DAY 1

DAY 2
Breakfast on your own
 7:45 a.m. Gather for bus departure
 8:00 a.m. Transfer to Ferreyra Industrial Park
8:30 A.M.
Ferreyra Industrial Park
A prominent industrial hub located in the City of Córdoba, Argentina. The Park hosts 
a diverse range of manufacturing operations. Companies with facilities in the area 
include Fiat Chrysler Automobiles (FCA), CNH Industrial, Promedon, and Dayco 
Argentina. 
 8:35 a.m. 
Promedon
Founded in 1985, Promedon is a leading private company in the research, 
development, production, and commercialization of medical technology for surgical 
procedures. With its headquarters in Argentina, Promedon has direct presence and 
subsidiaries in Argentina, Chile, Brazil, Mexico, and Germany. Promedon sells its 
products in over 60 countries
 8:35 a.m.
 
Site visit 
 
 
 9:50 a.m. 
 
 Group Photo
 9:55 a.m. Gather for bus departure
 10:00 a.m. Transfer to Helacor S.A. (Grido)
 10:20 a.m.
Helacor S.A. (Grido)
Founded in 2000, Helacor has transformed the ice cream industry in Latin America, 
producing the popular line of Grido ice cream. Helacor grew from a single store 
into a vast network of over 2,000 franchises. Internationally, the company has 
successfully expanded its business model and product offerings to Uruguay, 
Paraguay, Chile, and Peru.
 10:25 a.m. 
 Site visit and networking
 11:50 a.m. 
 
 Group Photo
 11:55 a.m. Gather for bus departure
 12:00 p.m. Transfer to Puerto Seco Córdoba
 12:10 p.m.
Puerto Seco Córdoba
Promoted by the Provincial Government, the ProCórdoba Agency, and the Córdoba 
Chamber of Foreign Trade (CaCEC), the Dry Port, located in the CaCEC Inland Port 
Terminal within the CaCEC Industrial Park, offers efficient logistics solutions to 
reduce costs, minimize road risks, decrease the carbon footprint, and strengthen 
the competitiveness of local exports.
 12:15 p.m. 
 Presentation
 12:35 p.m. 
 
 Group Photo
 12:40 p.m. Gather for bus departure
 12:45 p.m. Transfer to Stellantis
 12:50 p.m.
Stellantis
A multinational automotive manufacturing corporation formed in 2021 through the 
merger of Fiat Chrysler Automobiles (FCA) and the French PSA Group. The company 
encompasses 14 automotive brands. Its plant in Córdoba, located in Ferreyra, 
is a cornerstone of vehicle production in the region. Stellantis announced an 
investment of US$385 million, aimed at modernizing infrastructure and producing a 
new domestic engine and vehicle family. 
 
 12:55 p.m. 
Networking Lunch
 2:30 p.m. 
Site visit 
 3:40 p.m. 
 
 Group Photo
 
 3:45 p.m. Gather for bus departure
 3:50 p.m. Transfer to Club Atlético Talleres 
4:20 P.M.
High-Performance Center – 
Club Atlético Talleres
Club Atlético Talleres Founded in 1913, Club Atlético Talleres is one of the leading 
sports, social, and cultural institutions in Córdoba, serving as both an economic driver 
and a pillar of the province’s identity. With infrastructure that includes a modern high-
performance center, the club generates hundreds of direct and indirect jobs across 
multiple sectors.
 4:35 p.m. 
Site visit and Presentation
 5:40 p.m. 
 Group Photo

6:15 P.M.
Botanical Garden
 6:20 p.m. 
Presentation of the Center of Excellence in Circular 
Economy
Córdoba is home to the OAS Center of Excellence for Innovation and 
Transformative Technologies in the Circular Economy (CEITT EC) — the only 
one of its kind. This center connects global trends with local research and 
policy, strengthening regional networks on circular economy.
 6:50 p.m.

 Michael Bogachek, Executive Director, OAS

DAY 3
Breakfast on your own
 7:00 a.m. Gather for bus departure
 7:05 a.m. Transfer to Prodeman
9:40 A.M.
Prodeman
Prodeman S.A. is a family-owned agro-industrial leader based in General Cabrera 
founded in 1984. From its origins in peanut cultivation, the company has grown 
into a fully integrated enterprise that handles everything from farming and storage 
to processing, packaging and global export of peanuts and value-added peanut 
products. Prodeman exports to more than 40 countries around the world including 
The Netherlands, United Kingdom, Australia, Chile, Peru, Uruguay, among others.
 9:45 a.m. 
Site visit to Confectionery Peanut Plant 
and Blanched Peanut Plant

 2:45 p.m. Transfer to City of Río Cuarto
3:15 P.M.
Bio4
Based in Río Cuarto, the Bio4 specializes in producing bioethanol from corn, operating 
under a bioeconomy model that maximizes the use of every grain. The company 
maintains around 150 highly skilled direct and indirect jobs, employing engineers, 
biotechnologists, operators, and administrative staff. Its operations stimulate the 
regional economy by sourcing corn from local farmers while supporting the livestock 
industry through DDGS production (distillers dried grains with soluble, a high-protein 
animal feed, and corn oil for the food industry), strengthening the entire value chain 
in rural Córdoba.
 3:20 p.m.
Welcome Remarks
 Manuel Ron, President of the Innovar y Emprender Agency and 
Founder of Bio4
 Natalia Seiler, Executive Director of the Competitiveness Agency
 Tomás Beamonte, General Manager of Bio4

Agency

 University
5:45 P.M.
Río Cuarto National University 
(UNRC)
Established in 1971, UNRC is a leading public institution in the Province of 
Córdoba. Its academic community includes over 1,800 faculty members, nearly 
20,000 undergraduate students, and around 700 postgraduate students. The 
university offers 79 undergraduate and postgraduate programs, structured across 
five faculties: Agronomy and Veterinary Sciences; Economic Sciences; Exact, 
Physical, Chemical and Natural Sciences; Human Sciences; and Engineering.
 5:50 p.m.
Site visit 
School of Applied, Physical, Chemical and Natural Sciences, including the 
laboratory and the library.

DAY 4
7:00 a.m. Breakfast and Check-out
 7:50 a.m. Gather for bus departure
 8:00 a.m. Transfer to General Deheza
9:15 A.M.
Aceitera General Deheza (AGD)
AGD is an Argentinian-owned agro-industrial company headquartered in General Deheza, Córdoba. It produces natural 
foods, vegetable oils, protein flours, biodiesel, and refined glycerin. AGD operates in eight provinces of Argentina with 
seven industrial plants, AGD operates four main business units—Agriculture (UNAGRO), Brands, Peanuts, and Cereals & 
Oilseeds (UNCO)—covering everything from crop and livestock production to oil refining and consumer products, and 
exports to over 45 countries. Renowned for its integration, technological diversification, and sustainability practices, AGD 
has over 70 years of history, employs more than 2,700 people, and plays a leading role in Argentina’s agro-industrial sector.
 9:20 a.m. 
Site visit
 10:35 a.m. 
 Group Photo
 10:40 a.m. 
Networking Space 
 11:10 a.m. Gather for bus departure
 11:15 a.m. Transfer to City of Villa María
1:00 P.M.
Tonadita
Based in Villa María, Córdoba, Tonadita is a dynamic dairy company renowned for its high-quality products and continuous 
growth since its acquisition in 1998. With a modern 15,000 m² plant and a dedicated team of 250 employees, the company 
produces a wide range of dairy goods—including butter, cream, spreadable cheeses, dulce de leche, and specialty cheeses—
serving both South American and international markets. Tonadita has expanded steadily over the years, opening new 
plants, launching innovative products, and achieving certifications like FSSC 22000 to meet global standards. Its exports 
reach countries across the Americas, Africa, the Middle East, and Asia, with a recent successful entry into the U.S. market 
under strict FSMA compliance.
 1:05 p.m. 
 Networking Lunch in collaboration with Institute of Dairy Studies 
 (Escuela Superior Integral de Lecheria, ESIL)

DAY 5
7:00 a.m. Breakfast and Check-out
 7:55 a.m. Gather for bus departure
 8:00 a.m. Transfer to Costantini SA
8:15 A.M.
Costantini S.A.
An industrial automation company founded in 1993 specializing in custom 
robotic and automated solutions for sectors such as automotive, food, and 
manufacturing. Costantini designs and builds tailored systems for welding, 
cutting, packaging, and palletizing. The company serves clients across 
Argentina and exports to other Latin American markets, standing out for its 
ability to adapt technology to each customer’s needs.
 8:20 a.m. 
Site visit and Networking
 9:35 a.m. 
 Group Photo
 9:40 a.m. Gather for bus departure
 9:45 a.m. Transfer to Akron 
10:00 A.M.
Akron 
Akron produces self-unloading grain carts, grain baggers and extractors, seed 
and fertilizer wagons, forage compactor boxes, vertical feed mixers, and land 
levelers to efficiently support harvest, post-harvest, organic fertilization, 
and forage management tasks. Akron has presence in Canada, United States, 
Australia, Ukraine, Kazakhstan, South Africa, France, Uruguay, Paraguay and 
New Zealand.
 10:05 a.m. 
Site visit and Networking
 10:40 a.m. 
 Group Photo
 10:40 a.m. Gather for bus departure
 10:45 a.m. Transfer to San Francisco Industrial 
 
 
Park
11:00 A.M.
San Francisco Industrial Park
The San Francisco Industrial, Technological, and Logistical Park in Córdoba is 
one of Argentina’s largest and most dynamic industrial hubs, spanning 250 
hectares and hosting over 160 companies that generate around 3,000 jobs. 
Strategically located along key bioceanic corridors, it offers state-of-the-art 
infrastructure and services, including advanced utilities, logistics support, and 
business facilities. 
 11:05 a.m. 
Welcome Remarks
 Damián Bernarte, Mayor of the City of San Francisco
 11:15 a.m. 
Presentation

 1:00 p.m. Transfer to City of Arroyito
2:30 P.M.
Grupo Arcor 
Arcor is a leading Argentine multinational group specializing in food products, 
confectionery, chocolates, cookies, ice creams, agribusiness, and packaging. 
Founded on July 5, 1951, in Arroyito, Córdoba. Arcor operates over 40 industrial 
plants across Latin America, producing approximately 3 million kilograms of 
products daily. It is the world's leading candy manufacturer and one of the top 
producers of cookies and crackers in South America. The company exports its 
products to more than 120 countries and has commercial offices in America, 
Europe, and Asia.
 2:35 p.m. 
Site visit
 4:00 p.m. 
Networking Session
 4:25 p.m. 
 Group Photo
 4:30 p.m. Gather for bus departure
 4:35 p.m. Transfer to City of Córdoba
6:30 P.M.
Capitalinas
The Capitalinas building complex supports a dynamic business ecosystem. It 
serves as a strategic hub for technology, service, and consulting firms, attracting 
high value investments. Its prime location, linking Córdoba’s historic city 
center with major access routes, positions Capitalinas as a gateway for modern 
business and a symbol of the city’s economic diversification and dynamism.

 11:15 a.m. Transfer to Casa Naranja X
11:45 A.M.
Casa Naranja X
Naranja X is a leading Argentine fintech firm. Its app provides a full financial 
ecosystem—digital accounts, wallets, investments, loans, and virtual cards—
serving 1.8 million users, 10 million cards, and 450,000 businesses. With over 
3,000 employees, 170+ branches, Naranja X combines strong physical and digital 
presence while promoting access to financial services.
 11:50 a.m. 
Panel: Innovation Without Borders: How

 2:20 p.m. 
 Group Photo 
 2:25 p.m. Gather for bus departure
 2:30 p.m. Transfer to Globant
3:10 P.M.
Globant
Globant is a multinational company offering technology services, consulting, 
and software development. Founded in 2003, it offers a wide range of solutions, 
including software development, digital transformation consulting, AI-driven 
services, digital twins, mobile applications, and immersive experiences. Its 
innovative AI Pods model provides subscription-based AI solutions across 
industries such as entertainment, finance, and energy. With a presence in over 
30 countries and more than 31,100 employees, Globant is a leading exporter of 
knowledge-based services from Latin America, serving major clients including 
Google, Electronic Arts, Nissan, Banco Santander, and Formula 1, while generating 
most of its revenue outside Argentina.
